tokens: Jim, this is Houston with a request.

tokens: We're seeing some excursions in your Delta-P (pressure difference on) the Glycol Pump 1.
We'd like for you to pull the Auto Transfer circuit breaker on that Glycol Pump, and select Pump number 2.

tokens: Okay, I'm pulling Auto Transfer now; and I'm selecting Pump 2.

tokens: (Long Pause) Oh, boy; is that easy and fixed.

tokens: Okay, "PLSS LiOH cans, check the pins are in." (Pause) Green.

tokens: Don't have to put an LiOH (canister for the ECS) in it.

tokens: Just so there's plenty of food in it, Dave.

tokens: I'll give you some words shortly.

tokens: I'll call you before I get out.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Oh, I'm doing just fine, Joe.
I'm getting the high-gain antenna squared away for you, hopefully.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And while you're working there, Dave, you did such a beautiful job on fixing Jim's PLSS antenna, we've got another Walter Mitty repair job we'd like for you to carry out for us, please, on the cable lead from the high gain antenna to the LCRU, and when you think you're at a good place to do that, I'll go through a description of what we need.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And, Joe, I'm going to put...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: our camera keeps hanging up on that cable, and we'd like for you perhaps, if you think it's feasible, to tape a part of a cable below the fastening device on the staff of the high-gain antenna.
If you think a piece of tape right there might help, we suggest you go ahead and do that now.

tokens: Joe, I just completed that little task.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Joe, I'm in a position to get out.

tokens: Jim, the best I can do for you right now is ask you to stand by one minute.

tokens: I have a very keen sense of the obvious.

tokens: Okay, Jim, requesting you go back...

tokens: Copy that clearly, Dave, and when you are ready to drive the Rover, I've got some words to lay on you about getting those front wheels unstuck.

transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, circuit breakers are coming in.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, and the organic sample is closed for SRC 2.

tokens: And Mag Delta is on the 16 millimeter, and it seems to be working okay.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Joe, I'm in a position to take another crack at that steering, if you'd like to talk me through the procedures.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We want you to exercise the Forward Steering switch by cycling from Bus A to Bus C and back several times; and then stop with the switch finally at Bus Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Joe, I'm going to take bag number 3 off the back of the pallet.
Put number 6 there (as per checklist), so we can keep the numbers straight.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And Steering Forward is now in Bus Charlie, and I cycled it three times.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Now proceed on with your normal power-up cycle if you haven't already; and give me a call when you're ready to start driving.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Well, Jim still has a few things to do.
I just thought I'd take care of the other little sundry items if I could.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Basically, the rest of the procedure is just to cycle the Forward Steering circuit breaker, open; and then close, and then attempt Forward Steering.
You can do that when you power- up, and if there's no Forward Steering, we're going to ask that the Forward Steering switch be turned Off.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, Joe; I'm putting bag number 7 on the right-hand side of the tool carrier (as per LMP-4).
